title: Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX ITGC ITGC.ChangeMgmt.Testing — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX ITGC ITGC.ChangeMgmt.Testing (Change tested) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.12.8: Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.12.8' running on WinEventLog:Security, Active Directory replication/audit, snow:*, SAP/Oracle audit, mssql:audit, PAM, backup jobs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-180d type="emergency"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.8"
| eval start_epoch=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end_epoch=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval pi_ok=if(match(work_notes,"(?i)post.?implementation") OR match(close_notes,"(?i)PIR"),1,0)
| where pi_ok=0 AND state="Closed"
| table number, u_service_ci, start_date, state, short_description
description: Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX ITGC attestation.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOX ITGC compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart. Key fields: `end_epoch`, `number`, `pi_ok`, `short_description`, `start_date`, `start_epoch`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory

knownFalsePositives: Changes during scheduled month/quarter end, controlled refactoring, or audit-monitored access.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-180d type="emergency"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.8"
| eval start_epoch=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end_epoch=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval pi_ok=if(match(work_notes,"(?i)post.?implementation") OR match(close_notes,"(?i)PIR"),1,0)
| where pi_ok=0 AND state="Closed"
| table number, u_service_ci, start_date, state, short_description
```

Understanding this SPL

**Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness (SOX ITGC)** — Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX ITGC attestation.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:change_request, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **start_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **end_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pi_ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where pi_ok=0 AND state="Closed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Output fields: end_epoch, number, pi_ok, short_description, start_date, start_epoch, state, u_service_ci.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ERP audit trail (SAP, Oracle ERP), reconcile to ITSM or change records where needed, and confirm results, field extractions, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
grandmaExplanation: We align the evidence for emergency change retrospective documentation completeness the way our SOX and audit work expects, using the data feeds the control owners already have. SOX is about making sure financial systems stay trustworthy for reporting.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
